summary: The runtime architecture conforms to the run-session boundary: the one-command policy is callback-injected, the CLI remains an outer composition surface, the merged production connector and independent-review owners are reused rather than duplicated, and PR creation, merge, waiver, and material external side effects remain outside the autonomous action DAG. However, the exact-head pr-prepare acceptance_e2e_coverage diagnostic reports AC-1 through AC-8 as uncovered after the circular prose-only test was removed, because the remaining behavioral assertions do not carry machine-readable AC markers.

checks.reason: src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js owns policy inside the target model's run-session module and imports only node:timers/promises. Gate/readiness, PR preparation, runtime dispatch, polling, cancellation, and persistence are supplied through bounded callbacks.
No run-session implementation imports src/cli.js. src/cli.js remains the outer command/composition surface, and exact-head conformance records new_run_session_cli_reverse_dependency=false.

src/agent-runtime-connectors.js remains the production provider boundary from merged PR #377. The closure diff adds only the runtime objective to its existing prompt contract; it does not add another connector implementation.

src/independent-review-orchestrator.js remains the lifecycle, read-only identity, verdict, checkpoint, and recording owner from merged PR #382. guarded-run-session composes createGuardedIndependentReviewRunner and records through the existing Agent Review boundary instead of reproducing review policy in the one-command owner.

The branch is based on origin/main containing PR #384 Surface-aware Agent Review Freshness. Guarded review recording supplies inspection inputs and judgment deltas to the existing Agent Review recorder; unchanged-surface rebind requires an explicit assertion and matching inspection surface hash, while strict-head review remains fail-closed. The current main freshness regression matrix passes.

The autonomous registry contains only diagnose, prepare_artifacts, implement, verify, review, repair, and final_prepare. PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, and other material external side effects are absent and remain explicit human commands or typed approval stops.

Only final_prepare can return pr_ready, and both the action owner and the run-session binding require a prepared HEAD equal to the authoritative current managed-worktree HEAD with gate_status.ready_for_pr_create=true.

The four executable scenarios are substantive and non-circular, but current pr-prepare reports acceptance_e2e_coverage=needs_evidence with covered_acceptance_criteria_count=0 and AC-1 through AC-8 missing. File-header comments are not accepted as executable bindings; the relevant existing behavior assertions need AC marker text in their assertion messages or referenced local static bindings.

The exact-head conformance artifact compares against origin/main@118b68091b296f6bb8c4414cfc5eadb69ed73dc0 and improves violations from 81 to 80, delta -1, with no new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency.

findings.title: Behavioral E2E assertions lost machine-readable AC-1 through AC-8 bindings
findings.detail: The circular marker-only test was correctly removed, and the remaining four scenarios exercise real managed-worktree, review/repair, typed-stop/resume, and roadmap behavior. Nevertheless, .vibepro/pr/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure/pr-prepare.json records acceptance_e2e_coverage.status=needs_evidence, covered_acceptance_criteria_count=0, and every AC-1 through AC-8 as missing because no executable assertion message or assertion-referenced local static binding carries the corresponding marker.
findings.required_change: Add each AC marker to the message of an existing assertion that substantively proves that criterion, or to a local static binding consumed by that assertion. Re-run the four-scenario E2E and pr prepare, and require acceptance_e2e_coverage to cover AC-1 through AC-8. Do not restore a marker-only assertion or prose-only test.
